_comment: Group stage standings per FIFA World Cup. 'adv' = how many teams advance from each group. Teams listed in final standings order (1st→last). 'pts' uses 2-pts-per-win for 1930-1990, 3-pts-per-win from 1994 onward. Rounds array handles multi-group-round formats (1974, 1978, 1982).

1950.format: Groups (1 per group → Final Round Robin)
1950.note: Only the group winner advanced. Some groups had fewer than 4 teams due to withdrawals.

1954.note: Groups used a seeded system; seeded teams did not play each other. Ties broken by play-off matches.

1958.note: Some positions tied; play-offs held to determine qualifiers.

1974.format: Groups (2 per group) → Second Group Stage → Final
1974.note: Two group rounds replaced the traditional knockout bracket in 1974.

1978.format: Groups (2 per group) → Second Group Stage → Final
1978.note: Two group rounds again in 1978. Group winners of the second round met in the Final.

1982.format: 6 Groups (2 per group) → Second Group Stage (4 groups) → Semifinals
1982.note: 1982 had 24 teams split into 6 first-round groups; winners of the second round advanced to the semi-finals.

1986.note: 16 teams qualified for the knockout stage: 2 from each group plus the 4 best third-placed teams.

1994.note: First World Cup to use 3 points for a win.

2026.note: Tournament in progress. Standings updated from FIFA group tables (snapshot date: 2026-06-24).
